title: Communications Manager Job at Duob Recruitment
content:
(adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Duob Recruitment - Our Client is a social impact, investment & advisory group of companies with a focus on financial advisory, capital raising, and investment management services for institutions, governments, and government agencies. We are accretive to Africa's and Nigeria's economic development, targeting sectors such as infrastructure, renewable energy, SMEs, financial inclusion, healthcare and education. We partner with and serve communities, governments, and companies in Africa, providing an innovative mix of advisory, investment, research, analytics, and design services. Our business approaches problems differently and works together to create impact at scale. Our Client is a place where a diverse mix of the most talented individuals can bring their authentic selves to work and innovate — whether it's how you identify, where you come from, the languages you speak. Our Client is a home where people feel safe, understood, nurtured and encouraged to grow. We have very exciting, diverse projects across our portfolio companies ranging from a N35 bn Infrastructure Bond Issuance, a US$60 m renewable energy project, US$45 m education infrastructure, US$100 m Special Agro-Industrial Processing Zone amongst other projects.

They are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Communications Manager
Job Location: Abuja 
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	The Communications Manager leads the planning, development, and execution of communication strategies that strengthen Our Client portfolio companies brand reputation, visibility, and stakeholder engagement across all business units.
	The role oversees internal and external communications, public relations, digital media, content development, branding, and corporate storytelling.
	The ideal candidate is a strong strategist and storyteller who can translate complex, multi-sector work into clear, impactful messages that enhance the Group’s credibility and influence.


Key Responsibilities
Corporate Communications & Public Relations:


	Develop and implement integrated communication strategies aligned with Our Client mission and business objectives.
	Manage media relations, press releases, interviews, and public statements.
	Build strong relationships with media houses, journalists, influencers, and external partners.


Brand Management & Visibility:


	Strengthen and maintain consistent brand identity across all Our Client subsidiaries (Agro, Energy, Advisory, Capital, etc.).
	Oversee production of branded materials, corporate documents, and marketing assets.
	Ensure high-quality representation of the Group at events, conferences, and stakeholder engagements.


Digital & Social Media Management:


	Lead content strategy and management across social media platforms, website, newsletters, and online campaigns.
	Oversee creation of engaging content—articles, videos, graphics, reports, and human-interest stories.
	Track and analyze digital performance metrics to improve reach and engagement.


Internal Communications:


	Develop communication channels that support collaboration and alignment across Our Client teams and departments.
	Coordinate internal announcements, employee updates, and organizational messaging.


Strategic Support to Leadership:


	Prepare speeches, presentations, briefs, and communication materials for executives.
	Provide communication guidance during major projects, events, partnerships, and organizational changes.
	Support crisis communication and reputation management efforts.


Monitoring, Reporting & Continuous Improvement:


	Track brand visibility, media coverage, and campaign performance.
	Produce communication reports, insights, and recommendations for leadership.
	Implement communication best practices and stay updated with industry trends.


Required Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Communications, Public Relations, Mass Communication, Marketing, or related field.
	5–8+ years of experience in communications, PR, media, marketing, or corporate affairs.
	Master’s Degree is an added advantage.
	Proven experience managing corporate communication in a multi-sector or high-impact organization.
	Strong writing, editing, storytelling, and presentation skills.
	Experience developing and executing digital content strategies.
	Familiarity with design tools (Canva, Adobe Suite) is a plus.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Role Summary&nbsp;


	??We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Business Process Analyst to support the development, documentation and maintenance of business processes and compliance procedures.
	The ideal candidate will have strong analytical skills, excellent proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (especially Excel and Word), and experience with Power BI and Power Automate.?&nbsp;

	Experience with Power Automate for workflow automation.&nbsp;
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.&nbsp;

	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.&nbsp;
	Attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.?&nbsp;

Business Process Analyst Job at Tetra Maritime




        
    

        
        Tetra Maritime is a leading Nigerian ship owner and operator, providing comprehensive and tailor-made solutions across the oil and gas supply chain, including upstream, midstream, and downstream.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Process Analyst

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time (On-site)

Role Summary 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	??We are seeking a detail-oriented and proactive Business Process Analyst to support the development, documentation and maintenance of business processes and compliance procedures.
	The ideal candidate will have strong analytical skills, excellent proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (especially Excel and Word), and experience with Power BI and Power Automate.? 


Key Responsibilities 


	??Assist in the design, documentation, and implementation of business processes and procedures. 
	Collaborate with various departments to gather process requirements and ensure alignment with compliance standards. 
	Create and maintain process maps, SOPs, and workflow documentation. 
	Analyze existing processes to identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements. 
	Develop dashboards and reports using Power BI to monitor process performance and compliance metrics. 
	Automate routine tasks and workflows using Power Automate to improve operational efficiency. 
	Support internal audits and ensure documentation is up-to-date and accessible. 
	Provide training and guidance to teams on newly implemented processes. 
	Maintain version control and change logs for all process documentation?


Requirements


	??Bachelor’s degree. 
	Proven experience in process documentation and improvement. 
	Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Word. 
	Working knowledge of Power BI for data visualization and reporting. 
	Experience with Power Automate for workflow automation. 
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. 
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
	Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. 
	Attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.? 


     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Fleet Officer (Logistic) I",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Fleet Officer&nbsp;(Logistic) I

Job Location:&nbsp;Dangote Cement I Coast

Job Summary


	Provide support for the implementation of transport policies and processes by participating in planning/ coordinating the movement of cement products and/ or materials between the Plant and approved areas.
	Effective management of the drivers.


Key Duties and Responsibilties
Operational &amp; Functional:


	Manage vehicular traffic, including loading activities (off-loading and on-loading) at the Plant to ensure overall orderliness and efficiency of depot operations.
	Ensure appropriate storage of incoming materials at the designated facilities in the Plant.
	Coordinate loading of products onto allocated trucks for dispatch to approved locations outside the Plant.
	Ensure that all plant logistics activities comply with DIL&rsquo;s Health, Safety, and Environment standards.
	Coordinate efficient and timely loading of customers&rsquo; trucks with purchased products on a FIFO basis.
	Develop and submit reports of logistics activities at the Plant and provide recommendations where necessary.
	Ensure recovery of AGO for short kilometers.
	Ensure all vehicles are coming with raw materials from designated location whenever there&rsquo;s feasibility of collecting raw materials.
	Ensure timely allocation of vehicles for collection of spares.
	Liaising with Workshop and acquiring information on:
	Repair and release of Trucks
	Any other Emergency Occurrences
	Perform any other duties assigned by the Head of Department.
	Contributing to the overall performance of the service through the execution of the target objectives set for it&nbsp;
	Participate in all performance appraisals organized in the organization (mid-year and end-of-year review of the PMS)
	Attend training courses organized to strengthen skills.
	Perform any other duties as assigned by the Unit Head.


QSE Responsibilities:


	Know and fully apply DCC&#39;s QHSE requirements, policies, procedures.&nbsp;&nbsp;
	Take care of his own health and safety in the workplace and ensure that acts or omissions do not adversely affect the health and safety of other people.
	Take appropriate measures to minimize risks to quality, health and the environment.
	Communicate any case of hazardous situation to the Top management or QSE representative.
	Engage in the QHSE system by organizing all mandatory training and awareness&nbsp;
	Propose and carry out improvement, corrective and/or preventive actions.
	The list of missions described in this document is not exhaustive and is subject to change.&nbsp;&nbsp;


Key Requirements
Education and Work Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Transport &amp; Logistics, Mechanics Engineering or equivalent in a related discipline.
	Minimum of three (3) years of relevant work experience.&nbsp;
	Postgraduate/relevant professional qualification will be an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	In-depth understanding of transportation industry&nbsp;
	Knowledge on trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	Excellent knowledge and understanding of macro and micro environmental trends, and the impact on the organisation.
	Good knowledge of DCC&rsquo;s product and service offerings.
	Good research and data gathering skills.&nbsp;
	Good analytical and problem solving skills.
	Good business writing and presentation skills.
	Good networking and teaming skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity, honesty, reliability&nbsp;
	Ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Proficiency in the use of MS Office Word and Excel, SAP&nbsp;
	Fluent in both oral and written English;
	In-depth knowledge of local and DCC approved QHSE policies and norms
	Good knowledge of ISO standards.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-03-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-04-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Fleet Officer (Logistic) I Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Fleet Officer (Logistic) I

Job Location: Dangote Cement I Coast

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide support for the implementation of transport policies and processes by participating in planning/ coordinating the movement of cement products and/ or materials between the Plant and approved areas.
	Effective management of the drivers.


Key Duties and Responsibilties
Operational & Functional:


	Manage vehicular traffic, including loading activities (off-loading and on-loading) at the Plant to ensure overall orderliness and efficiency of depot operations.
	Ensure appropriate storage of incoming materials at the designated facilities in the Plant.
	Coordinate loading of products onto allocated trucks for dispatch to approved locations outside the Plant.
	Ensure that all plant logistics activities comply with DIL’s Health, Safety, and Environment standards.
	Coordinate efficient and timely loading of customers’ trucks with purchased products on a FIFO basis.
	Develop and submit reports of logistics activities at the Plant and provide recommendations where necessary.
	Ensure recovery of AGO for short kilometers.
	Ensure all vehicles are coming with raw materials from designated location whenever there’s feasibility of collecting raw materials.
	Ensure timely allocation of vehicles for collection of spares.
	Liaising with Workshop and acquiring information on:
	Repair and release of Trucks
	Any other Emergency Occurrences
	Perform any other duties assigned by the Head of Department.
	Contributing to the overall performance of the service through the execution of the target objectives set for it 
	Participate in all performance appraisals organized in the organization (mid-year and end-of-year review of the PMS)
	Attend training courses organized to strengthen skills.
	Perform any other duties as assigned by the Unit Head.


QSE Responsibilities:


	Know and fully apply DCC's QHSE requirements, policies, procedures.  
	Take care of his own health and safety in the workplace and ensure that acts or omissions do not adversely affect the health and safety of other people.
	Take appropriate measures to minimize risks to quality, health and the environment.
	Communicate any case of hazardous situation to the Top management or QSE representative.
	Engage in the QHSE system by organizing all mandatory training and awareness 
	Propose and carry out improvement, corrective and/or preventive actions.
	The list of missions described in this document is not exhaustive and is subject to change.  


Key Requirements
Education and Work Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Transport & Logistics, Mechanics Engineering or equivalent in a related discipline.
	Minimum of three (3) years of relevant work experience. 
	Postgraduate/relevant professional qualification will be an added advantage.


Skills and Competencies:


	Good understanding of trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	In-depth understanding of transportation industry 
	Knowledge on trends, challenges, opportunities, regulations and legislations relating to the cement manufacturing industry.
	Excellent knowledge and understanding of macro and micro environmental trends, and the impact on the organisation.
	Good knowledge of DCC’s product and service offerings.
	Good research and data gathering skills. 
	Good analytical and problem solving skills.
	Good business writing and presentation skills.
	Good networking and teaming skills.
	High ethical standards and integrity, honesty, reliability 
	Ability to manage multiple priorities.
	Proficiency in the use of MS Office Word and Excel, SAP 
	Fluent in both oral and written English;
	In-depth knowledge of local and DCC approved QHSE policies and norms
	Good knowledge of ISO standards.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior HSE Officer",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Senior HSE Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Dangote Cement I Coast

Job Summary


	Responsible for all aspects of OSHA safety programs, managing and/or supervising various projects while providing Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) guidance to company staff and contractors in order to achieve zero accidents/incidents and ensure full compliance with health, safety, and environmental legislation.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Develop and implement an HSE management system.
	Design and implement programs to raise employee awareness and ensure the practical application of health and safety practices in the workplace.
	Develop HSE policies and procedures in compliance with standards and legislation.
	Conduct accident and incident investigations and analyses and provide recommendations to prevent recurrence.
	Propose HSE capacity-building programs, such as defensive driving, OSHA training, etc.
	Liaise with external stakeholders on HSE matters.
	Provide an emergency response plan, test it, and update it as necessary.
	Provide guidance and advice to staff at all levels on HSE-related matters.
	Supervise HSE personnel.
	Perform special assignments and other related duties as required.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Proactive identification of workplace hazards and risk analysis to ensure workplace safety.
	Ensure compliance with legal requirements and DCC policies.
	Ensure effective implementation of HSE programs.
	Develop a positive Health, Safety, and Environment culture within DCCI.


Candidate Profile
Education and Professional Experience:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Health or relevant disciplines
	At least six (6) years of relevant professional experience in HSE or in a similar role.

Senior HSE Officer Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Senior HSE Officer

Job Location: Dangote Cement I Coast

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Responsible for all aspects of OSHA safety programs, managing and/or supervising various projects while providing Health, Safety, and Environment (HSE) guidance to company staff and contractors in order to achieve zero accidents/incidents and ensure full compliance with health, safety, and environmental legislation.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Develop and implement an HSE management system.
	Design and implement programs to raise employee awareness and ensure the practical application of health and safety practices in the workplace.
	Develop HSE policies and procedures in compliance with standards and legislation.
	Conduct accident and incident investigations and analyses and provide recommendations to prevent recurrence.
	Propose HSE capacity-building programs, such as defensive driving, OSHA training, etc.
	Liaise with external stakeholders on HSE matters.
	Provide an emergency response plan, test it, and update it as necessary.
	Provide guidance and advice to staff at all levels on HSE-related matters.
	Supervise HSE personnel.
	Perform special assignments and other related duties as required.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	Proactive identification of workplace hazards and risk analysis to ensure workplace safety.
	Ensure compliance with legal requirements and DCC policies.
	Ensure effective implementation of HSE programs.
	Develop a positive Health, Safety, and Environment culture within DCCI.


Candidate Profile
Education and Professional Experience:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Environmental Science, Occupational Health or relevant disciplines
	At least six (6) years of relevant professional experience in HSE or in a similar role.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Personal Assistant",
  "description": "Albacorre Consulting is a dynamic consulting firm dedicated to bridging talent with opportunity. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, high-quality solutions for clients and candidates alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Personal Assistant

Job Location:&nbsp;Chevron, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking a highly competent, polished, and proactive Executive Personal Assistant to provide direct support to the Creative Director.
	This role requires a high level of professionalism, discretion, organization, and the ability to manage both business and personal administrative tasks in a fast-paced interior design and project management environment.
	The ideal candidate must be smart, well-presented, confident, and able to take initiative without constant supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide executive and personal administrative support to the Creative Director
	Manage daily calendar, meetings, site visits, and appointments
	Coordinate communication with clients, contractors, vendors, and consultants
	Follow up on ongoing projects to ensure timelines are met
	Prepare reports, presentations, and official documents
	Manage emails, calls, and correspondence professionally
	Assist with project scheduling and task tracking
	Arrange travel, meetings, and event schedules when required
	Maintain strict confidentiality on all business and personal matters
	Run official and personal errands when necessary
	Support basic business coordination including social media, content, and brand activities when required
	Ensure the Creative Director&rsquo;s workflow is smooth and well organized at all times


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Management, or related field
	3&ndash;5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant / Personal Assistant
	Excellent spoken and written English
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	High level of professionalism and emotional intelligence
	Proficient in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and scheduling tools
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Must be smart, confident, and well presented
	Experience in interior design, construction, architecture, or real estate is a strong advantage


Skills &amp; Personal Qualities:


	Discretion and confidentiality
	Strong attention to detail
	Ability to take initiative
	Problem-solving mindset
	Time management
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;praise.isaiah@albacorrre.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Executive Personal Assistant Job at Albacorre Consulting




        
    

        
        Albacorre Consulting is a dynamic consulting firm dedicated to bridging talent with opportunity. We pride ourselves on delivering efficient, high-quality solutions for clients and candidates alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Personal Assistant

Job Location: Chevron, Lekki - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly competent, polished, and proactive Executive Personal Assistant to provide direct support to the Creative Director.
	This role requires a high level of professionalism, discretion, organization, and the ability to manage both business and personal administrative tasks in a fast-paced interior design and project management environment.
	The ideal candidate must be smart, well-presented, confident, and able to take initiative without constant supervision.


Key Responsibilities


	Provide executive and personal administrative support to the Creative Director
	Manage daily calendar, meetings, site visits, and appointments
	Coordinate communication with clients, contractors, vendors, and consultants
	Follow up on ongoing projects to ensure timelines are met
	Prepare reports, presentations, and official documents
	Manage emails, calls, and correspondence professionally
	Assist with project scheduling and task tracking
	Arrange travel, meetings, and event schedules when required
	Maintain strict confidentiality on all business and personal matters
	Run official and personal errands when necessary
	Support basic business coordination including social media, content, and brand activities when required
	Ensure the Creative Director’s workflow is smooth and well organized at all times


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Business Administration, Management, or related field
	3–5 years of experience as an Executive Assistant / Personal Assistant
	Excellent spoken and written English
	Strong organizational and multitasking skills
	High level of professionalism and emotional intelligence
	Proficient in Microsoft Office, Google Workspace, and scheduling tools
	Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
	Must be smart, confident, and well presented
	Experience in interior design, construction, architecture, or real estate is a strong advantage


Skills & Personal Qualities:


	Discretion and confidentiality
	Strong attention to detail
	Ability to take initiative
	Problem-solving mindset
	Time management
	Professional appearance and attitude
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 / Month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

     

    

        Click here to Apply
